author: barnabasbusa
content: could we actually keep the max beacon block size the same for minimal and mainnet? Feel like we would be doing a bunch of kurtosis tests later on to potentially test this edge case and would be a footgun to have different values for minimal. Or is this derived as of some formula ?

author: nc1234
content:

We are passing the minimal of each hard fork. Looks like mainnet gloas is still running.

author: jtraglia
content: There is a formula. It’s based on the actual maximum sizes given limitations.

author: jtraglia
content: Awesome 👏 yup for some reason the Gloas mainnet runner was killed after an hour and didn’t get notified until several hour later when heze finished. So I restarted that job and it appears to be working fine. Might be a sign of some oom issue, but hopefully it was just a fluke.

author: jtraglia
content: <@881905303011086387> can provide more information. But I think we should keep them. I have plans to simplify the way we handle configs/presets in the future (which would help with this) but it’s such a low priority task.

author: etan_status
content: because the static cap rather than the blob schedule of the current beacon clock is used instead etc

author: jtraglia
content: <@425572898787426305> <@504202741933932544> I took ideas from both of you & made these changes. What do you think? Is this simpler and more clear? https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5435/commits/fca1aa3f56c6e2f984b3a596aa18d748e3b1efbe

author: jtraglia
content: Essentially these changes:

author: tersec
content: checking

author: potuz
content: If you're going with prefix min and max I'd use the reservation part of my PR instead. At any rate uint is better than a bytes type to check for arithmetic inequalities

author: potuz
content: For your previous bit operations a byte type makes more sense

author: tersec
content: (been in meetings but don't want to hold this up, so will check before they wrap up)

author: jtraglia
content: To be clear, I'm trying to find a compromising balance from all of the feedback.

author: nflaig
content: isn't using `uint8` as version kinda ugly, if we reserve 0-15 now then the next builder (from a reserved range) in a future hard fork would need to be 16 or higher

author: potuz
content: I have no preference on the type or range per se. But I do on the semantics of the type. C++ won't even allow arithmetic operations on `byte`.

author: jtraglia
content: But changing it to uint8 just for that reason would be leaking language specific behavior. Would this affect golang?

author: jtraglia
content: If this is an issue, you could just do `if uint8(prefix) < uint8(BUILDER_WITHDRAWAL_PREFIX_MIN)` right?

author: jtraglia
content: If we cannot agree to something by the end of today, I don't think this will make it into the release.

author: m.kalinin
content: i am very sorry but i am busy with other things today, won't have time to review anything until tomorrow

author: tersec
content: mechanically, this implements an improvement on the status quo fine

author: jtraglia
content: No worries, all good!

author: tersec
content: I still find that it allocates a fairly large portion of "version space" for completely speculative reasons which it's going to be probably infeasible to reuse

author: tersec
content: but, there are multiple people (<@586161934425128960> and <@755590043632140352> for example) who seem convinced that this is a use case worth catering to. Unconvincing, but whatever. My main concern is that doesn't exhaust 1-byte version-space or similar

author: tersec
content: And regarding this, it doesn't have to 1:1 match any particular programming language's type system; it just has to be efficiently implementable

author: tersec
content: (and even more, I don't want to push to a 2-byte or larger space basically as a side effect of, oh, actually burning this many builder ID versions is a bit risky, i.e. pile complexity on complexity for a still-totally-abstract-and-vague set of concerns)

author: nflaig
content: we could just reserve `0xB1`, I don't think it's needs to be a range tbh

author: nflaig
content: especially because we could allow more in hegota if we need to

author: tersec
content: Yeah, ok, even if allowing for some "unspecified", I'd still want to limit that to exactly 1 that has no in-protocol semantics

author: tersec
content: because the semantic mapping has to be provided externally regardless

author: jtraglia
content: I'd be fine with setting `BUILDER_WITHDRAWAL_PREFIX_MAX` to 0xB1 too.

author: jtraglia
content: cc <@504202741933932544> as well (who just reviewed, thank you!)

author: nflaig
content: <@1035747431461167125> now you are in, voice your concern here 😄

author: nflaig
content: whatever makes Dustin happy

author: tersec
content: yeah, I'm happy enough with this

author: jtraglia
content: Hey <@1035747431461167125> what's your concern?

author: nc1234
content: I like the semantic of builder version being decoupled from the notion of withdrawal prefix except during the builder onboarding. To signal that not only it has different logic from validator's withdrawal prefix, but also the fact that version has nothing to do with withdrawal.

author: nc1234
content: But the more I read, the more I realize we are trying to strike a compromise here

author: jtraglia
content: Yeah I can relate. I also wanted these to be completely separate but you're right, it's a compromise.

author: tbenr
content: the same 🙂

author: jtraglia
content: Pushed this change. Thanks y'all. I think I'm going to merge these two in just a bit.

author: jtraglia
content: Cool. Will wait to merge that one until you've completed.

author: nflaig
content: just a final thought on that, if we wanna go with a new range or builder type that is currently reserved, wouldn't we anyways start with `0xC`?

author: nflaig
content: just semantics I guess, not sure matters either way

author: tersec
content: big-round-hex-numbers?

author: jtraglia
content: Only if we must guarantee there doesn’t currently exist a builder with that version. So most likely we’d just use 0xB1.

author: jtraglia
content: Or if we want to treat the leading nibble of the credential as a “builder type”. No strong feelings. We have plenty of options to do whatever we want.

author: tersec
content:
> Or if we want to treat the leading nibble of the credential as a “builder type”.

This is exactly the sloppy/wasteful/magic use of builder version space I'm concerned about

author: tersec
content: 256 isn't that big a number if one randomly gives away 16 here and there

author: nc1234
content: Just forming more opinions here: I think we are spending too much time speculating what future forks would look like and attempt to design this builder version to be ultimate future proof which isn't realistic. I think for Gloas, we can already see a list of potential Heze EIPs and AFAIK none of them require a new builder version and let alone the requirement of onboarding them pre-fork. If some PQ things really require new builder version say in I* (who knows, maybe later), then during Heze we would have see this coming and update Heze spec accordingly. I think we should just do 0xB0 in gloas. We should be able to look 1-2 hard forks ahead, this way we keep the design space open and be able to accomodate the future 1-2 forks' eips fully, rather than speculate and employ some mechanism that might not be effective. But if we are really paranoid, then I guess allowing 0xB1 deposit and forbid it from bidding in gloas is fine. In this case I would prefer 5435

author: jtraglia
content: Okay lol. Just throwing out that it’s an option, I’m not suggesting we do this. Let’s cross this bridge when we get to it.

author: jtraglia
content: Yeah I didn’t expect builder versioning to take up so much time. Sorry about that. Hoping what we have now is fine; strict enough and flexible enough. Let’s move on to other more important issues.

author: tersec
content: I agree with this, and it's my core discomfort with this approach, it's opening up a lot of random speculation about what we might want in 2 or 3 years with no evidence

author: tersec
content:
the thing is that the status quo in the spec is worse than 5435 -- i.e. shipping the spec as is is open-ended in a way that unfortunately isn't really
> I think we should just do 0xB0 in gloas

author: nflaig
content: I mentioned this before and was my original idea but might be to restrictive but we could literally just hard code to `PAYLOAD_BUILDER_VERSION` in`process_builder_deposit_request`, that way we also avoid using bytes for arithmetic checks

author: nflaig
content: and builders can't accidentally burn ETH becasue we never drop the deposit

author: nc1234
content: Yea "restricting now "is reversible (we can always relax in the future), but "permitting now" is not (some builders with unintended credentials already on chain, can't just kick them off the chain)

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5439  <@1035747431461167125> <@654267572107083777> essentially like that?

author: nflaig
content: could also return if `0xB0` is not withdrawal prefix, but not sure there's benefit to that

author: jtraglia
content: In my opinion, this is a bit too restrictive. I don't want to have to use the validator deposit contract to onboard some new type of builder before the fork; if that's something we need to do.

author: nflaig
content: well the argument is that from now until we ship heze we don't need that

author: jtraglia
content: That's true. I don't think we're going to add a new builder type in heze. But it feels wrong that we'd have to change this again in heze if we want to introduce something in I*.

author: nc1234
content: Why? We don't even know what EIPs are there for I* and we don't know what their requirement of builder is. We don't even know if they need to onboard the new builders pre-fork. We only know when we are writing Heze specs so change it then

author: jtraglia
content: I no longer have a strong opinion on this. We could do what <@586161934425128960> proposes and open it up later if necessary. According to the strawmap, mandatory proofs is now slated for K* which is pretty far out.

author: jtraglia
content: So I'll let you guys (client devs) decide. As a maintainer, I'm just going to ensure whatever we do is secure & fair.

author: wemeetagain
content: I think if we have a new type of builder, eg some proof builder, it may be better to just have a separate registry / lifecycle entirely. Or alternatively may not need to be onboarded before the fork. I don't think we have enough information now and so my preference is for the simplest approach. 🐒

author: wemeetagain
content: maybe even stricter, drop/reject deposits with non 0xB0 prefix

author: nflaig
content: I think that's good in a sense to lock in the `0xB0` as builder prefix

author: jtraglia
content: <@586161934425128960> <@534934855113506836> <@654267572107083777> <@412614104222531604> Let's move forward with Nico's PR but with Cayman's suggestion of really locking it down. It appears that no one agrees on how this works because we just don't have enough information right now. Let's defer this debate to a later upgrade.
